steps.title: Identify the starting value
steps.content: The function uses $x$ years after $2002$. At $x=0$, the estimated population is $3{,}500$, so the equation must start with $3{,}500$.

steps.title: Convert the percent increase to a multiplier
steps.content: A $4\%$ yearly increase means each year's population is $100\%+4\%=104\%$ of the previous year's population. As a decimal, the multiplier is $1.04$.

steps.title: Write the exponential model
steps.content: The model is $$p(x)=3{,}500(1.04)^x$$ This has initial value $3{,}500$ and multiplies by $1.04$ each year, so C is correct.
